title: pleiarina neowilsonii
intro: Pleiarina neowilsonii, also known as the "New Wilson's Pleiarina", is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a woody climber native to tropical regions of Asia, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The leaves and roots can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for consumption, and are also used in traditional remedies for their health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating pain.
